mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of industrial production correlate with stronger corporate earnings and broader economic output, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. This relationship holds across all phases of the business cycle.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO level and forward 20-day SPX returns is non-positive, or rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3%, it signals a tightening monetary regime that increases the discount rate on equity cash flows and reduces risk appetite, causing negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return following trigger events is non-negative, or more than 50% of events yield positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the ratio of retail sales to industrial production indicates that consumer demand is outpacing supply, signaling improved revenue and pricing power for firms, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the RSAFS/INDPRO ratio with 60-day forward returns is non-positive over a rolling 3-year window, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.

mechanism_narrative: During a period when the unemployment rate has risen for three consecutive months, an additional acceleration in consumer price inflation above 0.3% month-on-month signals stagflationary conditions that erode corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increasing 3 consecutive months and CPIAUCSL month-on-month > 0.3%
regime_hint: rising unemployment and high inflation regime
failure_condition: Over any 2-year window with at least 5 trigger events, the mean 20-day forward return is non-negative, or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5% within a month, it indicates a very tight labor market that increases wage pressures and reduces corporate profit margins, predicting negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day SPX return after trigger events is non-negative, or less than 60% of events are followed by negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales directly reflect increased consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year correlation between RSAFS month-on-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns is non-positive, or rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 3% and housing starts exceed 1 million units, accommodative monetary policy combined with strong housing demand fuels construction and consumption, generating positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Over any 5-year window with at least 10 trigger events, the average 60-day forward return is non-positive, or the win rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase characterized by rising GDP and falling unemployment, industrial production growth above its 6-month average signals robust demand and increasing capacity utilization, which predicts strong equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return after trigger events is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10), or rolling 3-year IC < 0.03.
